title: Placeholder Image Generator
shortDescription: Create placeholder images for wireframes, social cards, blog headers, and design systems.
metaDescription: Create custom placeholder images for mockups, headers, social cards, and design systems directly in the browser.
intro: Generate a placeholder image at the exact dimensions you need, then preview and download it as PNG or SVG.
overview:
Use this tool when you need a stand-in image with precise dimensions for layouts, content drafts, or interface concepts. It creates a simple graphic locally in the browser, so you can keep working without waiting for final artwork.

You can use it to mark empty image areas, test spacing around a hero section, or prepare a draft for a social share card. The output is meant to be lightweight and predictable, making it easy to swap out later for the final asset.

Because the image is generated from your chosen size and settings, it is useful for assets that must fit a specific container, from tiny thumbnails to wide banners.
howItWorks: Enter the target width and height.
Add optional label text or visual settings if the tool exposes them.
Generate the image in the browser.
Check the preview and download the file you need.

faq.question: Can I create a placeholder with a custom size outside standard presets?
faq.answer: Yes. Enter the exact width and height you need, even if it is an unusual ratio for a special layout.

faq.question: What happens if I choose very large dimensions?
faq.answer: The generator will still try to build the image, but very large canvases can be slower to render and heavier to download.

faq.question: Does the tool produce a transparent background or a filled one?
faq.answer: That depends on the available settings. If transparency is not selected, the image is rendered with the tool’s default background.

faq.question: Can I rely on the output as a final production image?
faq.answer: No. It is intended as a temporary stand-in for planning, not as finished artwork or brand imagery.

faq.question: Why does the output sometimes look different from the on-screen preview?
faq.answer: Minor differences can happen because the preview is resized for display, while the downloaded file uses the exact generated dimensions.

faq.question: What is the most common mistake when using this tool?
faq.answer: Entering the wrong aspect ratio. If the placeholder must fit a specific container, match the exact container dimensions before generating.
